Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotMediaOptions

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotMediaOptions
public class PotMediaOptions extends Object
A mutable builder for configuring playback and presentation options of a media element.

This class provides a fluent API to set various media properties such as autoplay, looping, volume, fade effects, trimming, poster frames, and bookmarks. Each configuration method returns the instance itself, allowing for method chaining. Values are validated using ValidationUtils where applicable.

Usage Example


 PotMediaOptions options = new PotMediaOptions()
     .autoPlay(true)
     .volume(0.8)
     .fadeIn(2.0)
     .fadeOut(1.5)
     .trim(5.0, 30.0)
     .posterFrame(imageBytes, "image/jpeg")
     .bookmark("Chapter1");
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    PotMediaOptions()
    Constructs a new PotMediaOptions instance with default values.

  • Constructor Details

    • PotMediaOptions

      public PotMediaOptions()
      Constructs a new PotMediaOptions instance with default values.

      Default values are: autoplay false, loop false, volume 1.0, hide icon false, full screen false, all fade and trim durations 0, and no poster frame or bookmark set.

  • Method Details

    • autoPlay

      public PotMediaOptions autoPlay(boolean autoPlay)
      Enables or disables automatic playback of the media.

      When set to true, the media will begin playing as soon as it is loaded and ready, without requiring explicit user interaction.

      Parameters:
      autoPlay - true to enable autoplay, false to disable it
      Returns:
      this PotMediaOptions instance for method chaining

    • loop

      public PotMediaOptions loop(boolean loop)
      Enables or disables looping of the media.

      When set to true, the media will restart from the beginning upon reaching its end, creating a continuous playback loop.

      Parameters:
      loop - true to enable looping, false to disable it
      Returns:
      this PotMediaOptions instance for method chaining

    • volume

      public PotMediaOptions volume(double volume)
      Sets the playback volume level.

      The volume is a value between 0.0 (silent) and 1.0 (maximum volume). The value is validated to be within this range using ValidationUtils.percentage01(double, String).

      Parameters:
      volume - the volume level, between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive
      Returns:
      this PotMediaOptions instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if volume is not between 0.0 and 1.0
      See Also:
      • ValidationUtils.percentage01(double, String)

    • hideIcon

      public PotMediaOptions hideIcon(boolean hideIcon)
      Controls the visibility of the media control icon.

      When set to true, the default media control icon (such as a play button overlay) will be hidden from view.

      Parameters:
      hideIcon - true to hide the icon, false to show it
      Returns:
      this PotMediaOptions instance for method chaining

    • fullScreen

      public PotMediaOptions fullScreen(boolean fullScreen)
      Enables or disables full-screen presentation of the media.

      When set to true, the media is intended to be presented in full-screen mode, typically occupying the entire display.

      Parameters:
      fullScreen - true to enable full-screen mode, false to disable it
      Returns:
      this PotMediaOptions instance for method chaining

    • fadeIn

      public PotMediaOptions fadeIn(double seconds)
      Sets the duration of the fade-in effect at the beginning of playback.

      The fade-in effect gradually increases the audio volume from silence to the set volume level over the specified duration. The value is validated to be non-negative using ValidationUtils.nonNegative(double, String).

      Parameters:
      seconds - the fade-in duration in seconds; must be >= 0
      Returns:
      this PotMediaOptions instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if seconds is negative
      See Also:
      • ValidationUtils.nonNegative(double, String)

    • fadeOut

      public PotMediaOptions fadeOut(double seconds)
      Sets the duration of the fade-out effect at the end of playback.

      The fade-out effect gradually decreases the audio volume from the set volume level to silence over the specified duration. The value is validated to be non-negative using ValidationUtils.nonNegative(double, String).

      Parameters:
      seconds - the fade-out duration in seconds; must be >= 0
      Returns:
      this PotMediaOptions instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if seconds is negative
      See Also:
      • ValidationUtils.nonNegative(double, String)

    • trim

      public PotMediaOptions trim(double startSeconds, double endSeconds)
      Defines a segment of the media to be played by specifying start and end trim points.

      Only the portion of the media between startSeconds (inclusive) and endSeconds (exclusive) will be played. Both values are validated to be non-negative, and endSeconds must be greater than or equal to startSeconds.

      Parameters:
      startSeconds - the start time of the trim segment in seconds; must be >= 0
      endSeconds - the end time of the trim segment in seconds; must be >= startSeconds
      Returns:
      this PotMediaOptions instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if startSeconds or endSeconds is negative, or if endSeconds < startSeconds
      See Also:
      • ValidationUtils.nonNegative(double, String)
      • PotException.invalidParameter(String, String)

    • posterFrame

      public PotMediaOptions posterFrame(byte[] imageData, String contentType)
      Sets a custom poster frame (thumbnail) image for the media.

      The poster frame is a static image displayed before the media begins playback or while it is loading. The image data and its MIME content type must be provided. Both parameters are validated for non-emptiness using ValidationUtils.

      Parameters:
      imageData - the byte array containing the image data; must not be null or empty
      contentType - the MIME type of the image (e.g., "image/jpeg", "image/png"); must not be blank
      Returns:
      this PotMediaOptions instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if imageData is null or empty, or if contentType is blank
      See Also:
      • ValidationUtils.notEmpty(byte[], String)
      • ValidationUtils.notBlank(String, String)

    • bookmark

      public PotMediaOptions bookmark(String bookmarkName)
      Associates a bookmark name with the media.

      The bookmark can be used to identify or reference this specific media configuration within the application. The name is validated to be non-blank using ValidationUtils.notBlank(String, String).

      Parameters:
      bookmarkName - the name of the bookmark; must not be blank
      Returns:
      this PotMediaOptions instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if bookmarkName is blank
      See Also:
      • ValidationUtils.notBlank(String, String)

    • isAutoPlay

      public boolean isAutoPlay()
      Returns whether autoplay is enabled.
      Returns:
      true if autoplay is enabled, false otherwise

    • isLoop

      public boolean isLoop()
      Returns whether looping is enabled.
      Returns:
      true if looping is enabled, false otherwise

    • getVolume

      public double getVolume()
      Returns the current volume level.
      Returns:
      the volume level, between 0.0 and 1.0 inclusive

    • isIconHidden

      public boolean isIconHidden()
      Returns whether the media control icon is hidden.
      Returns:
      true if the icon is hidden, false otherwise

    • isFullScreen

      public boolean isFullScreen()
      Returns whether full-screen mode is enabled.
      Returns:
      true if full-screen mode is enabled, false otherwise

    • getFadeInSeconds

      public double getFadeInSeconds()
      Returns the fade-in duration.
      Returns:
      the fade-in duration in seconds

    • getFadeOutSeconds

      public double getFadeOutSeconds()
      Returns the fade-out duration.
      Returns:
      the fade-out duration in seconds

    • getTrimStartSeconds

      public double getTrimStartSeconds()
      Returns the start time of the trim segment.
      Returns:
      the trim start time in seconds

    • getTrimEndSeconds

      public double getTrimEndSeconds()
      Returns the end time of the trim segment.
      Returns:
      the trim end time in seconds

    • getPosterFrameData

      public byte[] getPosterFrameData()
      Returns the poster frame image data.
      Returns:
      the byte array containing the poster frame image data, or null if not set

    • getPosterFrameType

      public String getPosterFrameType()
      Returns the MIME content type of the poster frame.
      Returns:
      the content type of the poster frame (e.g., "image/jpeg"), or null if not set

    • getBookmarkName

      public String getBookmarkName()
      Returns the associated bookmark name.
      Returns:
      the bookmark name, or null if not set

    • hasPosterFrame

      public boolean hasPosterFrame()
      Checks whether a poster frame has been set.

      A poster frame is considered set if both posterFrameData and posterFrameType are non-null.

      Returns:
      true if a poster frame is set, false otherwise

    • hasBookmark

      public boolean hasBookmark()
      Checks whether a bookmark has been set.
      Returns:
      true if a bookmark name is set (non-null), false otherwise

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this PotMediaOptions instance.

      The string includes the values of all playback-related fields (autoplay, loop, volume, etc.), but excludes poster frame data and bookmark name for brevity.

      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string representation of the object
